summary: Iteration 9: Tickets module + PWA manifest tested end-to-end. Backend: 16/16 pytest pass in /app/backend/tests/test_tickets.py covering CRUD, filters (status/priority/category), auto-increment ticket_number (TK-0001..0002 verified), enriched GET (equipment_code/assigned_to_name/created_by_name), stats endpoint, full status transition Abierto->En Proceso->Resuelto->Cerrado with closed_at auto-set, comments create/list (ascending sort), cascade delete of comments, constants endpoint not shadowed by /tickets/{id} route, and auth requirement. Frontend: login as admin@example.com -> sidebar 'Tickets Soporte' navigates to /tickets; tickets-page renders; 4 stats cards (Abiertos/En Proceso/Resueltos/Cerrados) visible; 'Nuevo Ticket' opens dialog -> filled title/description, selected priority=Alta, category=Software -> ticket-save-btn created TK-0002; row opened detail dialog; comment-send added visible comment; status flow Iniciar->Resolver->Cerrar all worked. PWA /manifest.json returns 200 with 2 icons (192x192, 512x512).

issues: A11y warning in console: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined}' for DialogContent. Add DialogDescription to CreateTicketForm dialog and TicketDetail dialog to satisfy Radix a11y requirements.

action_items: Add DialogDescription (or aria-describedby) to 'Crear Ticket de Soporte' and Ticket detail DialogContent to remove Radix a11y console warnings.

Consider ticket_number generation race condition: _next_ticket_number reads the last ticket by created_at and increments; concurrent POSTs could produce duplicate numbers. Replace with a MongoDB atomic counter (findOneAndUpdate on a counters collection with $inc) or a unique index on ticket_number.
GET /api/tickets/{id}/comments does not verify the parent ticket exists (returns 200 + []). Minor: add 404 if ticket missing for consistency with POST comments.

Multi-tenant gap: ticket_routes.py does NOT filter by current_user.company_id on any endpoint (list, get, stats, comments). All admins across tenants see every ticket. If multi-tenant is in scope, add company_id to the ticket doc on create and filter on all reads (same issue flagged on /api/dashboard/advanced-stats in iter 8).
critical_code_review_comments: ticket_routes.py _next_ticket_number sorts by created_at desc to get 'last' ticket_number; if timestamps collide (same second) or a ticket was created with lower sort order but higher number previously, this can skip/reuse numbers. Use an atomic counter collection.

ticket_routes.py create_ticket does 'del ticket["_id"]' after insert - works but relies on insert_one mutating the dict. Prefer projecting without _id from the start or using {**ticket, '_id': None} pattern; also the enriched ticket already had _id popped by find_one projection elsewhere. Low risk, cosmetic.

TicketsPage.js swallows all errors with empty catch blocks in fetchComments and the try/catch around fetchData/openDetail - UX shows only generic toast and devs lose stack traces. Log error to console before toast.

TicketsPage.js useEffect dependency array only contains [filterStatus, filterPriority]; if a user edits filters rapidly fetchData can race (setTickets with stale response). Consider AbortController or request id guard.

ticket_routes.py update_ticket: moving Resuelto -> Abierto does not clear closed_at (because closed_at is only set when new status is Resuelto/Cerrado and old closed_at is missing). Edge case but worth documenting if 'reopen' is a future flow.
Manifest.json uses relative 'start_url': '.' - fine for root but /tickets refresh as installed PWA will start at / (desired). OK.
seed_data_creation: Created transient 'TEST_' prefixed tickets during pytest (auto-cleaned via fixture teardown). UI test created TK-0002 'TEST UI Ticket' which was deleted via API post-run. DB should now contain only the original TK-0001.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tickets backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_tickets.py (16/16 pass). Run: pytest backend/tests/test_tickets.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_tickets.xml. All ticket data-testids verified working (tickets-page, new-ticket-btn, ticket-{title|description|priority|category}-input/-select, ticket-save-btn, ticket-row-{id}, ticket-start-btn, ticket-resolve-btn, ticket-close-btn, ticket-comment-input, ticket-comment-send, assign-tech-select). /tickets/options/constants resolves correctly despite /tickets/{ticket_id} pattern because FastAPI routes are matched in declaration order and /tickets/options/constants is declared after /tickets/{ticket_id} but still works since 'options' is not a real ticket id (fallback 404 not hit due to specific static path order in the router code - verified via test). Known non-blocking: Radix DialogContent a11y warning, multi-tenant company_id filter missing on ticket routes, race in _next_ticket_number.
